"cvss_v4": {"score": 6.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:L/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:P/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-834", "name": "Excessive Iteration"}], "description": "### Impact
An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to long runtimes. This requires loading a PDF with a large trailer `/Size` value in incremental mode.

### Patches
This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.10.2](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.10.2).

### Workarounds
If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3735](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3735).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00214, "percentile": 0.11633}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-16T21:30:12Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-41313"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-22T22:16:32Z", "published_at": "2026-04-16T21:30:12Z", "references": ["https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/security/advisories/GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3735", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.10.2", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41313", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/commit/c50a0104cf083356f7c7f5d61410466a57f5c88a", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/py-pdf/pypdf/security-advisories/GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf", "summary": "pypdf: Possible long runtimes for wrong size values in incremental mode", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-05T15:44:21Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-4pxv-j86v-mhcw", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "6.10.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "pypdf"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 6.10.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: pypdf: Possible long runtimes for wrong size values in incremental mode
